mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2 percent within a single quarter, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.2%, or the predictive t-statistic for the threshold signal falls below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, a sustained month-over-month increase in real consumer credit balances demonstrates resilient household spending that offsets tightening headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative predictive relationship when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, or forward 60-day returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling RSAFS periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over a one-quarter horizon as corporate cash flows scale with aggregate economic output.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns exhibit a correlation coefficient below 0.15 over a 10-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100.0 while the CPI index remains above 310.0, it confirms a stagflationary supply shock that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional rank correlation between INDPRO and forward SPX returns remains positive during low production periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while real consumer credit simultaneously contracts signals a shift from debt-fueled consumption to wage-driven spending, which historically supports corporate earnings stability and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the cross-lag correlation between PAYEMS acceleration and RSAFS deceleration shows no predictive power for equity returns (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is rising but the unemployment rate remains anchored below 4.0 percent, equity markets price in a soft-landing scenario that sustains valuation multiples and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE stays below 4.0% during rate hikes, or the forward return differential between soft-landing and hard-landing regimes falls below 1.0% (p > 0.05).
